name: Fed Hold + Elevated US CPI
description: The Federal Reserve holds the policy rate at 3.50–3.75% on the same day US CPI prints 4.2% YoY — the highest reading since April 2023. New Chair Kevin Warsh gives his first post-decision press conference. The CBUAE mirrors the hold, keeping the base rate at 3.65%.

expected.primary_signal: Fed hold + CPI 4.2% → EIBOR locked at 5.80% all-in
required_calculations: EIBOR transmission: current ADS on AED 5m reference loan

scoring_notes.mandate_relevance: A strong brief ties the rate hold explicitly to SME debt serviceability across all 5 sectors, not just generic 'rates stay high'.
scoring_notes.quantitative_accuracy: The ADS calculation must use EIBOR 3.80% (Base Rate 3.65% + ~15bps) + 200bps spread = 5.80% all-in, quarterly amortisation over 7 years on AED 5m principal.
scoring_notes.data_integrity: FRED API key missing in this environment; a strong brief flags this gap and states which values were sourced via web search instead.
scoring_notes.action_specificity: MONITOR is appropriate — no immediate debt-service stress, but watch Warsh's press conference for dot-plot revision that could shift EIBOR path.
